diagnosis: Three records matched the frozen route but used an unregistered synonym or broader phrase for the residual.
One record differed on both the non-release route name and the residual phrase.
One record preserved the exact residual but selected a different non-release route name.
The v1 contract incorrectly treated exact free-form snake_case wording and a fine-grained route name as semantic identity.
The concise reasons appear directionally relevant on manual inspection, but that post-outcome observation is diagnostic only and cannot rescore the frozen result.
repair_requirements: Create a new protocol identity and freeze it before a new response.
Separate a small canonical decision class from free-form route detail.
Use a closed residual-class vocabulary visible to the candidate while keeping task-to-label mappings evaluator-only.
Preserve the v1 raw response, result, costs, and failure label unchanged.
Do not open the difficulty sweep or confirmatory denominator until the new sacrificial protocol independently passes.
